new_classes.label_en: Other still wine (no PDO/PGI/variety/vintage), in containers > 10 l (CN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (ohne g.U./g.g.A./Rebsorte/Jahrgang), in Behältnissen mit mehr als 10 l (KN 2204 29 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes, not bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO/g.U.) or protected geographical indication (PGI/g.g.A.) and not identified by grape variety or vintage year, in containers holding more than 10 litres, as classified under CN subheading 2204 29 90. The total dry extract is composed of non-volatile substances including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring agents and salts, as defined in Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for CN 2204 29 90 (within the range 22042922–22042998) refers to Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, which define total dry extract (Gesamttrockenstoff) and enumerate its non-volatile components (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring agents, salts). The following criteria are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204); (2) container size > 10 l (LargeContainerOver10L); (3) no PDO, no PGI, no variety/vintage designation (NoPDONoPGINoVarietyNoVintageWineQuality); (4) not sparkling / not semi-sparkling (complement restrictions); (5) total dry extract quality referenced (TotalDryExtractQuality). Coverage is not complete (score 0.75) because: (a) the specific dry-extract thresholds and their numeric values from Additional Note 4 are not yet formally modelled as OWL datatype restrictions in the TBox (no named dry-extract sub-quality classes with numeric facets are available); (b) the distinction between alcoholic strength bands (e.g. ≤ 15% vol vs > 15% vol) which may further subdivide this residual 'other' code cannot be fully resolved without inspecting sibling codes at this level – the 22042990 code is the catch-all 'other' residual, so the main discriminating criteria (large container > 10 l, no PDO/PGI/variety/vintage, not sparkling/semi-sparkling) are the principal sufficient conditions. The note text is primarily a guidance cross-reference to the chapter additional notes rather than introducing new product-discriminating criteria beyond what is already encoded.
